filesCreated.purpose: Frontend JavaScript to cap .top-gradient max-height to nearest <main> element height, with ResizeObserver for dynamic updates

syncStatus.notes: CSS change (150vw -> 150vh) applies to both editor and frontend. JS is frontend-only via Hero.js (bundled into screen.js by webpack glob pattern). No TSX/PHP changes required.

testPage.notes: No block markup changes. Existing test page is still valid and does not need to be updated.

fixesApplied.change: Changed height: 150vw to height: 150vh on .top-gradient rule

Created new frontend JS file. Queries all .top-gradient elements on DOMContentLoaded, finds each one's nearest <main> ancestor (or falls back to document.querySelector('main')), sets max-height to main.offsetHeight, and attaches a ResizeObserver to update max-height whenever main resizes. Multiple hero blocks on the same page are each handled independently via querySelectorAll iteration.

output.implementationNotes: The JS was implemented as a standalone Hero.js file (bundled into screen.js via webpack glob) rather than as inline PHP script output. This approach avoids PHP function validator false-positives on JavaScript identifiers, follows the established pattern used by QuickNavigation.js, and cleanly handles multiple hero instances on the same page via querySelectorAll.
